text:
Village Institutes were a key element of educational reform during the Republican era in Turkey. The first Village Institute was opened in 1940 with the main aim of training the teachers who would be sent to villages to improve the conditions of the peasants. With the increasing health needs of the nation and changes in state policy, the need for a professional health staff was realised. As a result the Health Branch was introduced to the Village Institutes in 1943, to train village health assistants who, together with the rural teachers, would work to improve the living conditions of the rural peasants.

The chapter investigates the careers and education of colonial medical officers by relying on archival and oral histories. Field research involved the interviewing of four retired VHOs who were amongst the last to have graduated in 1951, their testimonies being written down in great detail. In addition, personal documents belonging to these VHOs were also collected for the first time, these consisting of student identification cards, colonial medicine textbooks of the time, medical school graduation certificates, certificates awarded by Sahra Medical Service School as well as permit for anaesthesia training.

Village Health Officers’ Education and Role in Rural Health Care This study reconstructs the training that the villagers received through the data obtained from the primary sources by combining it with information from the literature and legislative materials. It reveals that the training given to the village health officers was state-of-the-art training meeting all the health requirements that were faced in rural Anatolia. Furthermore, they were active in health campaign work for the eradication of contagious diseases. The aim of this paper is to shed light to lesser-known aspects of the era and contribute to the historical literature by acting as a filter through which it is possible to understand the approach to education, health policies and the societal structure in a very critical period in the history of the Turkish nation.
